description: Adversarial invariant for issue #54: ordinary CRISPR-Cas9 editing requires PAM compatibility, not later engineered PAM-specificity variants.

checks.reason: Engineered PAM-specificity variants are later optimizations, not prerequisites for ordinary CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing.
CRISPR-Cas9 editing must retain the basic PAM compatibility constraint.
PAM compatibility is a hard Cas9 target-recognition dependency.
Engineered PAM variants should remain downstream of ordinary CRISPR-Cas9 editing.
CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing should remain earlier than engineered PAM variants.
Engineered PAM-specificity variants should remain a later optimization node.
